picks.name: NVIDIA Corporation
picks.rationale: NVDA remains the irreplaceable spine of the AI infrastructure supercycle — FY2026 revenue of $215.94B (+65% YoY), ROIC of 104.67%, and a forward P/E of ~20x with a PEG of 0.45 make this far less stretched than headline multiples imply. Today's pullback to ~$197 (well off the $236.54 52-week high) with the stock sitting 16% below peak represents a technically healthier entry point versus the extended momentum flags from prior weeks. Druckenmiller's memory-chip rotation and Berkshire's GOOGL add both implicitly underwrite continued AI capex, the single biggest demand driver for NVDA's data-center GPU franchise. With 62 analysts at a Strong Buy and a consensus PT of $298.93 (+49% from current), the asymmetry clearly favors the long. The July 28-29 FOMC live-hike risk is the key near-term overhang, but NVDA's revenue is driven by multi-year contracted hyperscaler orders, not short-cycle demand.
picks.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings (expected late August 2026) — consensus is looking for continued data-center revenue acceleration; any guidance above $45B+ would force another wave of upward estimate revisions
picks.entryNote: Trading at ~$197, approximately 17% below the 52-week high of $236.54; forward P/E ~20x vs. FY2026 EPS growth of 65%+ — one of the cheapest PEG ratios (0.45x) in the large-cap universe; consolidating above the $193 intraday low support
picks.exitTrigger: Hyperscaler (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, or META) meaningfully cuts 2027 AI capex guidance, or NVDA issues a revenue warning signaling order pushouts from any of its top-5 customers
picks.keyRisk: AI capex circular-financing bust — if one or more hyperscalers cuts capex guidance materially, NVDA's demand visibility evaporates and the stock could re-rate to 12-15x forward earnings, implying 30-40% downside

picks.name: Meta Platforms, Inc.
picks.rationale: META is the single most attractively valued mega-cap AI name at ~19x forward P/E versus a peer median of ~25x, with FCF generation that dwarfs most software peers. The thesis just gained a new monetization vector: as of July 1 2026, Meta was reported to be forming a new cloud business to sell its surplus AI compute capacity, turning capex spend into a revenue line and directly challenging AWS/Azure/GCP in enterprise AI inference. AI-driven ad targeting improvements are already compounding into top-line growth, and the momentum screen awarded META a 22/25 composite score with pristine OBV accumulation signals. Ackman's 15.71% Uber weight alongside his MSFT/AMZN positions confirms the concentration-of-conviction into platform-network businesses; META fits the same template with superior FCF metrics.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (expected late July) plus formal announcement of the cloud compute business — dual catalysts could reprice the stock from 19x toward the 23-25x peer median
picks.catalystWindow: Late July 2026 (earnings) + July–September (cloud business launch details)
picks.entryNote: META trades at ~19x forward P/E vs. a 5-year average of ~22x and peer median of ~25x; XLC is the worst-performing sector YTD (-9.8%) which has compressed META's near-term price action despite fundamentals improving — creating a valuation gap vs. intrinsic value; stock pulled back with broader tech on July 1
picks.exitTrigger: Revenue growth deceleration below 15% YoY for two consecutive quarters, or regulatory break-up action that separates Instagram/WhatsApp, destroying the cross-platform ad targeting flywheel
picks.keyRisk: European or US regulatory antitrust action forcing structural separation of the family of apps, which would destroy the unified data-targeting moat

picks.name: Alphabet Inc.
picks.rationale: Berkshire added +224% to GOOGL in Q1 2026, making it a ~$16.6B position — the largest percentage buy Greg Abel made in his first quarter as CEO, and the strongest single institutional endorsement in the entire 13F cycle. GOOGL trades at a discount to MSFT on AI/cloud despite Google Cloud's accelerating growth trajectory and DeepMind's model superiority in several benchmarks. Alphabet joined the Dow Jones Industrial Average on June 29, 2026, replacing Verizon — a forced institutional buying event that adds persistent index-demand support. The stock's YTD underperformance relative to NVDA/AVGO creates a catch-up opportunity as the market rotates from momentum semis toward quality-compounding platforms heading into Q2 earnings.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (expected late July) — Google Cloud growth rate above 35% YoY and any AI monetization update on Gemini integration into Search/Workspace would be the key re-rating trigger
picks.entryNote: GOOGL trades at approximately 20-22x forward earnings vs. MSFT at 28x+ — despite comparable or superior AI infrastructure (TPUs, DeepMind); Berkshire's +224% add confirms the 'cheap vs. peers' valuation argument at current levels; recent Dow inclusion creates structural index-demand support
picks.exitTrigger: Google Cloud loses market share for two consecutive quarters to Azure/AWS, or DOJ antitrust remedy forces Search divestiture or Firefox/Apple distribution agreement termination
picks.keyRisk: DOJ antitrust remedy in the Search monopoly case — forced behavioral changes to distribution agreements could meaningfully impair Search revenue and trigger a multi-turn P/E de-rating

picks.name: Broadcom Inc.
picks.rationale: Broadcom is the cleanest expression of the custom-silicon AI ASIC cycle — Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue hit $10.8B (+143% YoY) with management guiding $16B in Q3 and ~$56B for FY2026 (+180% YoY). AI booking backlog of $30B vs. $10.8B shipped provides exceptional forward revenue visibility through 2028. The stock is down 20%+ from June highs on guidance interpretation concerns, creating a technically healthier re-entry relative to the May peak — this is the opposite of an 'extended' condition. Druckenmiller initiated AVGO as his largest Q1 purchase by dollar value, a high-conviction signal from a manager who was simultaneously rotating out of commodity tech into picks-and-shovels AI infrastructure. Operating margin of 67.3% and FCF of $10.3B in a single quarter underpin the quality overlay.
picks.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings (expected September 2026) — AI semi revenue of $16B+ vs. Q2's $10.8B would represent the largest single-quarter jump in the company's history and force another round of upward revisions
picks.entryNote: Trading 20%+ below June highs; AI segment bookings ($30B) vastly exceed current-quarter shipments ($10.8B), creating a visible revenue ramp not yet reflected in current price; Druckenmiller's new position is a fresh institutional endorsement at these levels
picks.exitTrigger: Any of AVGO's top-3 hyperscaler ASIC customers (Google TPU, Meta MTIA, or a third unnamed hyperscaler) announces a pivot to NVIDIA GPUs or in-house chip design that displaces AVGO custom silicon
picks.keyRisk: Custom ASIC commoditization — if hyperscalers shift back to merchant silicon (NVDA) due to cost or performance parity, AVGO's AI-segment revenue ramp stalls and the premium valuation cannot be sustained

picks.name: S&P Global Inc.
picks.rationale: SPGI is the highest-quality compounder in the Financials sector — a ratings-and-data duopoly (with Moody's) that benefits from structural growth in bond issuance, ESG data demand, and AI-driven analytics licensing. It has been a consistent top-5 holding in the growth book across seven consecutive trading days, and the thesis remains intact. In a higher-for-longer rate environment, bond issuance activity is structurally elevated as corporates refinance and sovereigns fund widening deficits — the US fiscal deficit at $1.9T (5.8% of GDP) alone is a multi-year tailwind for SPGI's ratings revenue. Unlike banks, SPGI has zero credit risk on its balance sheet; its economics are fee-based with 80%+ gross margins and consistent double-digit EPS growth. The company is also a direct AI data beneficiary — its Marketplace and commodity analytics platforms are being integrated with LLM tooling, opening new revenue streams.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (expected late July) — bond issuance volume data and any guidance upgrade on AI-data licensing revenue would be the key re-rating event
picks.entryNote: SPGI trades at approximately 28-30x forward P/E, consistent with its 5-year historical range; elevated bond issuance from corporate refinancing and sovereign deficit financing continues to provide a structural tailwind to the ratings business that is underappreciated at current multiples
picks.exitTrigger: Regulatory breakup of the SPGI/Moody's ratings duopoly, or a significant and sustained decline in bond issuance activity driven by a sharp drop in corporate refinancing needs
picks.keyRisk: Regulatory attack on the ratings duopoly structure — any legislative effort to introduce mandatory rating rotation or fee-cap on credit ratings would structurally impair SPGI's core ratings economics

picks.name: RTX Corporation
picks.rationale: RTX is the premier defense-aerospace compounder and a multi-year hold with NATO rearmament and Middle East tension as secular tailwinds. The defense sub-sector sits in the Tier 1 'HIGH CONVICTION' slot of the sector scorecard, and Industrials remain in the Leading quadrant of the momentum map as of July 1. RTX uniquely benefits from both the defense supercycle (Raytheon Missiles & Defense backlog >$60B) and the commercial aerospace recovery (Pratt & Whitney GTF engine program ramping). The July 1 WTI crude price drop below $70/barrel — while a headwind to energy — is actually a tailwind for RTX's commercial aerospace customers, improving airline economics and accelerating narrow-body aircraft orders. Elliott Management's campaign against Honeywell's aerospace division validates that standalone defense/aerospace assets are being re-rated by activists, supporting the sector multiple.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (expected late July) — Pratt & Whitney GTF powder-metal engine inspection program resolution update and any upward revision to FY2026 free cash flow guidance would be the key catalyst
picks.entryNote: RTX trades at approximately 20-22x forward earnings, at or slightly below its defense-sector peer median; XLI is up 24.7% over the past year and RTX has been a consistent top performer within it; today's oil price drop below $70 provides a net-positive read-through for commercial aerospace margins
picks.exitTrigger: Pratt & Whitney GTF inspection reveals a materially broader defect scope than disclosed, forcing a full fleet grounding and generating liability exposure above $5B, or a DOGE-driven DoD budget cut that eliminates one or more major Raytheon contract programs
picks.keyRisk: Pratt & Whitney GTF powder-metal disk defect liability creep — any expansion of the inspection scope or unexpected fleet groundings by major airline customers would re-introduce significant unquantified liability into the earnings model

picks.name: Micron Technology, Inc.
picks.rationale: Micron achieved the highest composite momentum score (24/25) in the entire universe, with Q3 FY2026 EPS of $25.11 crushing the $20.20 consensus by +24.3%, record gross margins of 85%, HBM3E/HBM4 fully booked through 2027 with demand extending into 2028, and $22B in strategic customer agreements including $18B in cash deposits. Druckenmiller initiated a new MU position in Q1 2026 as part of his memory-cycle inflection trade. The extended flag from prior weeks is partially mitigated by today's broader tech pullback — NVDA's intraday range of $193-$199 suggests the semi complex is consolidating rather than breaking down. The AI memory scarcity thesis (HBM is ~6x the ASP of standard DRAM) is a multi-year structural story, not a short cycle.
picks.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings (expected September 2026) — any HBM4 design win announcement or guidance for FY2027 HBM revenue exceeding $30B+ would confirm the cycle is extending, not peaking
picks.entryNote: MU has pulled back from its momentum peak; the +268% YTD run creates extension risk but the PEG of 0.10x and HBM4 booking coverage through 2028 mean fundamentals continue to grow into any valuation concern; consolidation above key support levels is the appropriate technical condition to maintain the position
picks.exitTrigger: HBM demand pause signaled by Samsung or SK Hynix discounting HBM3E pricing by more than 15%, or any major AI hyperscaler announces a shift to an alternative memory architecture that reduces HBM content-per-GPU
picks.keyRisk: Memory cycle turns suddenly — if AI training workloads plateau and inference proves less HBM-intensive than expected, DRAM pricing reverts to historical cyclical patterns and MU's 85% gross margins compress rapidly back to 40-50%

picks.name: Exxon Mobil Corporation
picks.rationale: XOM is the highest-quality expression of the energy sector — disciplined capex, the strongest free cash flow yield among majors, and a direct hedge against the geopolitical Middle East inflation risk that the macro briefing identifies as a top-3 tail risk. Today's WTI drop to ~$70 (Strait of Hormuz normalization) is a near-term headwind that is already partially priced; the structural bull case rests on AI data-center power demand (natural gas), the HALO rotation thesis ('Heavy Assets, Low Obsolescence'), and energy's confirmed Tier 1 status on the momentum map as of June 30. XOM's Pioneer acquisition integration is generating synergies ahead of schedule, and the Permian production ramp gives volume growth even in a flat-price environment. Forward P/E of ~11-12x vs. 20x for the S&P 500 provides a significant valuation buffer.
picks.catalyst: July 3 June NFP report — if non-farm payrolls come in weak (consistent with today's 98K ADP miss), stagflation narrative intensifies, re-anchoring energy as the inflation hedge of choice for institutional portfolios
picks.entryNote: XOM at ~$107-110 range; trading near 11-12x forward earnings vs. 10-year average of ~13x; WTI at $70 is near the lower bound of the current range and below OPEC+ fiscal breakevens, suggesting supply discipline will re-emerge; Strait of Hormuz normalization reduces geopolitical premium but structural AI power demand and energy transition capex remain
picks.exitTrigger: WTI crude sustains below $60/barrel for more than 6 consecutive weeks, implying a demand shock that destroys the FCF thesis, or Permian production guidance cut by more than 10% on operational issues
picks.keyRisk: Oil price collapse driven by a global growth shock — if the late-cycle macro regime accelerates into recession faster than expected, WTI could break below $60 and destroy XOM's FCF-based valuation support

picks.name: Quanta Services, Inc.
picks.rationale: PWR is the dominant contractor for electric grid infrastructure, transmission lines, and renewable energy interconnection — the physical layer that enables the AI data-center power buildout. It has been a top-10 holding in the growth book for seven consecutive sessions, and the thesis is structurally intact: AI data centers require 10-100x more power than conventional server farms, and the US transmission grid is critically underbuilt for this load. Structural trends in power demand, infrastructure, and electrification are identified as a decade-long capex cycle in the sector scorecard, with PWR as the primary direct beneficiary of every utility dollar spent on grid expansion. ISM Services at 54.5 and the JOLTS openings at 7.6M confirm the services economy is strong enough to sustain infrastructure spend despite rate headwinds.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (expected late July/early August) — any acceleration in 12-month backlog growth above 15% YoY, driven by new hyperscaler data-center interconnection contracts, would force consensus estimate revisions sharply higher
picks.entryNote: PWR has consolidated after a strong run; Industrials sector remains in the Leading momentum quadrant as of July 1; valuation at approximately 30-33x forward P/E is premium but justified by the multi-decade infrastructure capex cycle; today's broader market pullback offers a marginally better entry than prior days
picks.exitTrigger: Federal permitting reform reversal that halts interstate transmission line approvals, or a hyperscaler publicly announces a shift to on-site microreactors (SMRs) eliminating the grid interconnection need for new data centers
picks.keyRisk: Federal permitting bottlenecks — if the NEPA reform process stalls in Congress or courts, transmission project timelines extend by 3-5 years, reducing PWR's near-term backlog conversion and re-rating the stock toward a lower-growth multiple

picks.name: Vistra Corp.
picks.rationale: VST is the rare momentum/value hybrid that the momentum screen identified as a standout — strong fundamental revisions, 20-year power purchase agreements totaling 3,800 MW with Meta and AWS, trailing ROE of 81.09%, and yet the stock still trades near a 52-week low, meaning price has NOT run ahead of fundamentals. It is the direct beneficiary of AI data-center power demand via long-term contracted revenue, making it effectively a toll-road on the AI buildout without semiconductor execution risk. Today's news that Meta is forming a new cloud business to sell surplus compute only deepens Meta's energy procurement needs — and VST is one of Meta's primary contracted power suppliers. In the late-cycle/stagflationary macro regime, regulated and contracted power generation is the most rate-insensitive form of utility revenue, directly addressing the IEF/TLT headwind that plagues traditional utilities.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (expected late July/early August) — any announcement of additional long-term PPA contracts with hyperscalers or data-center developers, or an upward revision to FY2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance, would be the trigger
picks.entryNote: VST trades near its 52-week low despite Q1 2026 revenue rising to $5.63B with 26.6% operating margin and 20% EBITDA growth YoY; this price-fundamentals disconnect — where the stock is at lows while earnings are at highs — is the definition of a value entry in a momentum-saturated market; forward P/E significantly below sector peers
picks.exitTrigger: One of VST's major PPA counterparties (Meta or Amazon) terminates or restructures its long-term contract before 2030, reducing contracted revenue visibility by more than 20%, or Texas ERCOT regulatory changes impose price caps that structurally reduce merchant power margins
picks.keyRisk: ERCOT price cap risk — Texas regulators could respond to elevated power prices driven by data-center demand by imposing capacity market reforms that reduce VST's merchant energy price realization, directly impairing the FCF thesis

picks.name: SPDR Gold Shares ETF
picks.rationale: GLD is the portfolio's primary tail-risk hedge against the two scenarios where everything else in the book goes wrong simultaneously: (1) AI capex bust forcing a sovereign fiscal crisis and USD debasement, and (2) Middle East Strait of Hormuz re-escalation spiking energy and locking the Fed into a policy error. The US fiscal deficit at $1.9T (5.8% of GDP) growing to $3.1T by 2036, with debt-to-GDP rising to 120%, is a structural multi-decade gold bull catalyst. Starboard Value added 11% to its IAU (gold ETF) stake as of July 1 — institutional defensive hedging is creeping up. With real policy rates barely positive (Fed funds 3.625% mid vs. PCE at 4.1%), gold's opportunity cost remains historically low.
picks.catalyst: June CPI on July 14 — any headline print above 4.5% would spike gold as markets price near-certain September rate hike + fiscal credibility concerns simultaneously
picks.entryNote: Gold has already broken out secularly; GLD provides portfolio insurance against the exact tail risks identified in the macro briefing (AI capex bust, Hormuz re-escalation, fiscal dominance); Starboard's IAU add and the pattern of institutional defensiveness confirms the position
picks.exitTrigger: Federal Reserve pivots to aggressive rate cuts (implying a hard landing, which would actually be positive for gold initially) OR the US enacts credible multi-year fiscal consolidation reducing the deficit trajectory below 3% of GDP
picks.keyRisk: USD strengthens sharply on risk-off flight to quality — in a sudden risk-off event, the dollar and gold can temporarily move in the same direction, but historically gold recouples to its fiscal/debasement drivers within 1-3 months

picks.name: Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF
picks.rationale: SCHD provides broad diversified exposure to high-quality dividend payers with strong free cash flow, low payout ratios, and above-market yield — a direct expression of the HALO ('Heavy Assets, Low Obsolescence') rotation thesis that has taken hold in 2026. In a late-cycle regime with the Fed on hold and hike risks rising, dividend compounders with pricing power (energy, industrials, consumer staples embedded) outperform on a risk-adjusted basis. SCHD has no single-stock concentration risk, screens out value traps via FCF payout ratios, and provides meaningful income in a 4%+ yield environment where T-bills compete for short-duration cash. The ADP miss today (98K vs. 112K consensus) reinforces the consumer strain thesis, which historically accelerates flows from speculative growth into dividend quality.
picks.catalyst: July 3 NFP report — a weak payrolls print would confirm the ADP signal and accelerate institutional rotation from growth to dividend quality; SCHD typically outperforms in the 2-4 weeks following a dovish labor surprise
picks.entryNote: SCHD has been a consistent Tier 2 defensive holding throughout the prior 7 sessions; current yield approximately 3.5-3.8%, which provides a meaningful income buffer relative to the 4.37% 10Y Treasury but with equity upside participation; consolidating near multi-month highs as the rotation into defensives accelerates
picks.exitTrigger: Fed cuts rates to below 2.5% in a recession scenario, collapsing dividend spreads vs. Treasuries and triggering a sector rotation back into rate-sensitive growth — at that point SCHD would be replaced with pure equity beta
picks.keyRisk: Dividend traps — in a severe recession, even quality dividend payers cut dividends; SCHD's FCF-screening methodology mitigates but does not eliminate this risk for the portfolio's most cyclically exposed holdings

picks.name: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. Class B
picks.rationale: BRK.B at $397.4B in cash as of March 31, 2026 is the ultimate portfolio option on market dislocation. It is effectively a call option on the AI capex bust or any other risk-off scenario where Berkshire's unlevered, insurance-diversified business model and cash fortress allow Greg Abel to acquire assets at distressed prices. It correlates negatively with the speculative AI names in the growth book, provides low beta, and has actual earnings power from GEICO, BNSF, BHE, and its equity portfolio (now including GOOGL +224%). The $397.4B cash pile — up 6.5% from December — signals that Abel considers current market valuations too rich for large-scale deployment, exactly the optionality the defensive sleeve needs.
picks.catalyst: Any major market dislocation event (AI capex warning, FOMC hike shock, Hormuz re-escalation) that creates a buying opportunity for Berkshire — Abel has explicitly been positioned to deploy capital opportunistically
picks.catalystWindow: Ongoing; next major disclosure at August 14, 2026 13F filing
picks.entryNote: BRK.B trades at approximately 1.4-1.5x book value vs. the S&P 500 at 20x forward P/E — a relative valuation extreme that historically resolves through BRK.B outperformance in risk-off regimes; the new GOOGL +224% position confirms Abel's willingness to take concentrated bets when conviction warrants
picks.exitTrigger: Berkshire begins deploying cash aggressively at scale (>$100B in 90 days) into equities — that signal paradoxically means the market has dislodged enough that you want more direct equity beta, reducing the need for the BRK.B defensive wrapper
picks.keyRisk: Berkshire-specific key-man risk reduction — Greg Abel is a capable operator but the premium ascribed to BRK.B's portfolio management judgment was built over decades under Buffett; any evidence that Abel's capital allocation judgment differs materially could compress the book-value premium

picks.name: iShares 7-10 Year Treasury Bond ETF
picks.rationale: IEF is the portfolio's pure negative-correlation instrument — the one position that gains most decisively in the scenario where the AI capex thesis cracks and the Fed is forced into emergency rate cuts. With the 10Y at 4.48% today (up from 4.37% just days ago) and the July FOMC now 36.3% priced for a hike, IEF carries real duration risk in the base case (hawkish Fed). However, it is sized at only 4% precisely because it is insurance, not a core bet. The medium conviction reflects the real tension: in a soft-landing scenario with the Fed hiking, IEF underperforms; but in any hard-landing scenario or AI-capex bust, IEF provides the most immediate and liquid hedge, delivering 7-8% price appreciation per 100bps of rate decline.
picks.catalyst: July 14 June CPI — a sub-4% print (aided by the WTI drop below $70 today) would sharply reprice September hike probability lower and send IEF 2-3% higher in a single session
picks.entryNote: IEF at ~$95-96 range with the 10Y at 4.48%, near multi-month yield highs — this is the best entry point for IEF in months; sized at only 4% as tail insurance with asymmetric upside in the hard-landing scenario; medium conviction reflects the base-case hawkish Fed headwind
picks.exitTrigger: 10Y Treasury yield breaks above 5.0% on a sustained basis (more than 2 weeks) with core PCE re-accelerating above 4.5% — at that point the duration pain exceeds the hedge value and IEF would be replaced with shorter-duration T-bills
picks.keyRisk: Sticky inflation forces the Fed to hike more than currently priced — every 25bps of unexpected rate hikes costs IEF approximately 1.5-2% in price, and a full 100bps hiking cycle would generate a 6-8% loss on this position

picks.name: The Coca-Cola Company
picks.rationale: KO provides the consumer staples exposure that the defensive sleeve requires — the July 1 sector rotation update formally upgraded Consumer Staples to Tier 1-2 Rising, with Walmart and Costco reaching all-time highs and the sector confirmed in the Leading momentum quadrant. KO specifically offers pricing power in a 4.2% CPI inflationary environment (has raised prices 8 consecutive years), a 3%+ dividend yield, zero balance sheet leverage concerns, and Berkshire Hathaway's long-standing endorsement as a core holding. The ADP miss (98K vs. 112K) and consumer strain thesis in the macro briefing accelerates the defensive appeal of non-discretionary beverage demand — Coke's volumes are historically counter-cyclical. In a stagflationary overhang, KO is one of the few businesses that can simultaneously pass through cost inflation and maintain volume, making it a rare earnings-stability anchor.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (expected mid-July) — organic revenue growth confirmation above 8% and any FY2026 EPS guidance raise would confirm KO's pricing power thesis in the current inflationary environment
picks.entryNote: KO has been a periodic defensive sleeve member across the last 7 sessions (appearing on June 24, 25, and 30); trades at approximately 21-23x forward earnings, consistent with its defensive-staple premium; Consumer Staples sector just reached all-time highs confirming the rotation into defensives is institutionally supported
picks.exitTrigger: Organic volume growth turns negative for two consecutive quarters (implying GLP-1-driven behavioral shift away from sugary beverages is materializing faster than expected), or cost input inflation (aluminum, HFCS, water) structurally compresses gross margins below 55%
picks.keyRisk: GLP-1 secular volume headwind — if Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk's obesity drugs achieve 30%+ US adult penetration by 2030, Coke's carbonated beverage volumes face a structural multi-year decline that pricing increases cannot fully offset

summary: The July 1, 2026 portfolio is constructed around a late-cycle stagflationary regime: the Fed holds at 3.50-3.75% with a hawkish dot-plot flip (36.3% July hike probability post-hot PCE), core PCE at 3.8% YoY, WTI crude just broke below $70 on Hormuz normalization, and the ADP miss of 98K confirms consumer strain is building. The growth book concentrates in AI infrastructure (NVDA, AVGO, MU — three complementary value-chain nodes), high-quality platform compounders with defensive FCF characteristics (META, GOOGL, SPGI), real-asset beneficiaries of the AI power buildout (PWR, VST), defense/aerospace (RTX), and energy (XOM) as the inflation hedge and HALO-rotation play. All ten growth names are direct carryovers from the prior 7-session positioning history — thesis integrity maintained with zero churn — with VST elevated in rank given today's META cloud compute announcement deepening its contracted-revenue visibility. The defensive sleeve (GLD, SCHD, BRK.B, IEF, KO) is sized at 24% of the total book, reflecting the defensiveScore of 4 in a market that remains constructive on earnings but faces meaningful rate and geopolitical tail risk into the July 28-29 FOMC. The dominant risk being underwritten is that this portfolio is heavily exposed to continued AI capex cycle validity — an NVDA/AVGO/MU cluster that collectively represents the AI infrastructure spend-cycle's biggest single-quarter earnings driver.
diversificationNote: The growth book spans five distinct GICS sectors — Information Technology (NVDA, AVGO, MU — capped at 3 per sector rule), Communication Services (META, GOOGL — 2 names sharing digital-platform exposure but with distinct revenue drivers: social advertising vs. search/cloud), Industrials (RTX, PWR — defense aerospace and grid infrastructure, complementary not correlated), Energy (XOM — upstream FCF compounder), and Utilities (VST — contracted power, not rate-sensitive traditional utility); the two Communication Services names and three Information Technology names represent the maximum allowed concentration but are deliberately chosen across complementary value-chain exposures (custom silicon vs. GPU vs. memory vs. ad platform vs. cloud search); the defensive sleeve spans four distinct exposures: gold commodity hedge (GLD), broad dividend-quality equity (SCHD), conglomerate optionality/cash fortress (BRK.B), intermediate duration Treasuries (IEF), and consumer staples pricing-power anchor (KO) — covering all required defensive exposure types.

picks.name: NVIDIA Corporation
picks.rationale: NVDA sits at the apex of the AI infrastructure supercycle with FY2026 revenue of $215.94B (+65% YoY) and $120B in earnings — exceptional fundamentals supported by the Blackwell and Vera Rubin architecture ramp. The macro briefing confirms hyperscaler capex approaching $800B in 2026, a direct order-book tailwind for NVDA's data center segment. Today's ~$195 price sits ~17% below its all-time high of $236.54 reached May 14, and 62 analysts carry a consensus $298.93 target (+52% upside), making this a buy-the-dip entry within an intact secular trend. Persistent top-holding in this book for 7 consecutive sessions — thesis unchanged.
picks.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings report featuring Vera Rubin ramp revenue disclosure and updated hyperscaler order commitments
picks.entryNote: Trading ~$195 vs. 52-week range $151–$237; ~17% off ATH, consolidating above $192 support; EPS TTM $6.53 implies ~30x trailing — elevated but justified by 65%+ revenue growth; consensus 12M target $298.93
picks.exitTrigger: Gross margin compression below 70%, confirmed hyperscaler capex pause or material in-house chip substitution (AMZN Trainium, GOOGL TPU) displacing Blackwell orders
picks.keyRisk: Hyperscaler capex rationalization or export control escalation interrupting China demand and global order flow

picks.name: Meta Platforms Inc.
picks.rationale: Meta has been a top-4 holding in this book for 7 consecutive sessions — thesis fully intact. The company is a direct AI monetization compounder: advertising revenue is accelerating on AI-personalization gains, Llama 4 models underpin platform efficiency, and Reality Labs losses are stabilizing. The sector rotation scorecard flags Communication Services as 'coiling' with ad markets improving, while META's FCF generation, share buyback velocity, and expanding AI-driven ad CPMs make it the cleanest quality compounder in the XLC space. Core PCE at 3.4% and resilient payrolls (+172K May) support sustained advertiser spending, the primary revenue driver.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings with AI-driven ad revenue growth and Llama monetization update
picks.entryNote: Trading near multi-month highs following consolidation; P/E ~27x on accelerating EPS trajectory; ad-market tailwinds from resilient labor market (unemployment 4.3%) underpin near-term estimates
picks.exitTrigger: Ad revenue growth decelerating below 15% YoY or Llama competitive moat eroding to closed-source rivals (GPT-5/Claude 4) on enterprise adoption
picks.keyRisk: Regulatory antitrust action on Instagram/WhatsApp integration or AI content liability legislation

picks.name: Alphabet Inc.
picks.rationale: Berkshire Hathaway's Greg Abel added ~40M shares to bring total to ~57.8M shares worth ~$16.6B in Q1 2026 — the most consequential smart-money endorsement of the quarter. GOOGL trades at ~26x P/E (cheapest Mag-7), while Google Cloud is approaching 20%+ operating margins and Search monetization continues to prove structurally resilient. Here is your full macro briefing as of **Friday, June 26, 2026**:

---

## 📊 US EQUITY REGIME BRIEFING — SENIOR MACRO STRATEGY
*Prepared for: Long-Only Portfolio Manager | Date: June 26, 2026*

---

### DATA DASHBOARD

**FED POLICY**


The Fed held the federal funds rate unchanged at **3.50%–3.75%** for a fourth consecutive meeting in June 2026.
 
June 17, 2026 was the first meeting under new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh.
 
The updated dot plot reflects a hawkish pivot, with a **3.8% median rate projection for 2026**.
 
For the end of 2026, most officials now expect the benchmark rate to sit between **3.6% and 4.1%** (up from their previous estimate of 3.25%–3.75%).
 
Fed rate expectations remain elevated, with **markets pricing in ~3 rate hikes** this year, and the probability of the first move in September standing at around **62%**.
 
The longer-run neutral rate anchor has a heavy concentration of participants pinning it exactly at **3.0%**
 — meaning the current policy rate at 3.625% midpoint sits approximately **+62 bps above neutral**, a meaningfully restrictive real posture given headline PCE already at 4.1%.

**YIELD CURVE**


As of market close June 25, 2026, the US Treasury yield curve is upward-sloping: **2Y = 4.13%**, **10Y = 4.40%**, **30Y = 4.86%**.
 
The **2s10s spread = +27 bps** and the **3m10y spread = +62 bps**.
 Both are firmly positive after a prolonged inversion. 
Elevated term premium and steady Treasury issuance continue to support longer maturities, aligning with the view that benchmark yields stay higher for longer.


**INFLATION**


May CPI came in at **4.2% YoY**, up from 3.8% in April — the **third straight month of accelerating headline inflation**.
 
Energy is most of the heat: the conflict with Iran and disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz pushed energy prices up **23.5%** over the past year.
 
Core CPI, however, rose just **0.2% MoM** — half of April's pace — with its annual rate at **2.9%**.
 
The May PCE print showed **headline PCE at 4.1%** and **core PCE at 3.4%** — the highest core since 2023 and well above the Fed's 2% target, though both matched forecasts.
 Regime characterization: **energy-driven re-acceleration in headline, sticky-but-contained core** — a bifurcated inflation picture that is politically difficult for the Fed to look through.


Fed officials revised their median core PCE forecast for 2026 to **3.3%** (vs. 2.7% in March), and headline PCE to **3.6%**.
 
This reflects a consensus that the downward trajectory of inflation in this cycle will be slower than previously anticipated.


**GROWTH**


GDP growth for 2026 is now projected at **2.2%** (down from 2.4% in March).
 
May payrolls came in at **+172K** vs. an +80K consensus; revisions added **+93K** to prior two months, with unemployment holding at **4.3%**.
 
The FOMC statement noted economic activity is "expanding at a solid pace" with "productivity growth and capital investment strong."
 
Wages are the one clear soft spot, with annual growth easing to **3.4%**.


**CREDIT & LIQUIDITY**


**HY OAS = ~275 bps** (as of early June 2026)
 — 
down ~14% over the past year and well below the long-term average of 393 bps and the historical median of 452 bps.
 
Broad **IG OAS is approximately 80 bps** — near 25-year tights.
 Credit is screaming risk-on, but at these levels leaves little cushion for spread widening if growth softens. 
The 10Y yield is **4.39%** on the week — down 7 bps.


**EARNINGS & VALUATION**


The S&P 500 trailing earnings yield stands at approximately **3.98%** as of June 23, 2026
, implying a trailing P/E of roughly **~25x**. 
With the 10Y yield at 4.40%, the earnings yield is **below** Treasury yields, suggesting stocks are not cheap relative to bonds by historical standards.
 
The yield gap (earnings yield minus 10Y Treasury) is currently approximately **–0.37%**, about 0.30 standard deviations below the historical average — indicating equity markets are near fairly valued but offer no margin of safety vs. fixed income.


**GEOPOLITICAL TAPE**


The US and Iran have agreed to a provisional peace deal, though oil prices remain above pre-war levels, while recent labor market data has pointed to continued employment strength.
 
Geopolitical tensions resurfaced this week following an attack on a cargo ship near Oman in the Strait of Hormuz.
 
The US and Iran signaled progress in talks after their memorandum of understanding halted the naval blockade, and tanker flows through the Strait of Hormuz have picked up.


---

## ¶1 — REGIME CALL

**Late-Cycle, Elevated-Inflation Variant.** This is a late-cycle regime defined by the simultaneous presence of above-neutral real policy rates (FFR midpoint ~3.625% vs. neutral 3.0%), re-accelerating headline inflation driven by a geopolitical energy supply shock (headline PCE 4.1%, headline CPI 4.2% YoY), 
a hawkish dot-plot pivot toward a 3.8% median 2026 rate with policymakers raising inflation forecasts significantly and expecting to keep rates high for a prolonged period
, and a labor market that remains resilient but is no longer the source of wage-push pressure (
wages decelerating to 3.4% YoY
). The curve has re-steepened positively (2s10s +27 bps, 3m10y +62 bps) — historically this signals early recovery *or* fiscal/term premium stress; in this context, given HY OAS near multi-decade tights and equities near full valuation, it reflects the latter. The bull case (mid-cycle extension) rests entirely on the Iran ceasefire holding and energy disinflationary spillover into core — the base case is that the Fed is boxed in, policy easing is deferred well into 2027, and the cycle clock is ticking.

---

## ¶2 — WHAT'S WORKING / WHAT'S BREAKING

**Working:** Energy & Commodities (direct beneficiary of Strait of Hormuz premium; energy up 23.5% YoY), Defense (geopolitical re-armament cycle), Quality Large-Cap Tech with AI revenue — 
capital investment is strong
 and AI infrastructure capex continues to support Mega-Cap earnings. Financials benefit from a steepening curve (2s10s back to +27 bps from deep inversion) expanding net interest margins. Short-duration, high-quality IG bonds now offer competitive real returns. **Breaking:** Rate-sensitive sectors are under pressure — homebuilders, REITs, and small-cap growth are impaired by higher-for-longer rates. 
Inflation has proven sticky over the past few years, with both CPI and PCE still well above the Fed's 2% target
 — this kills the "soft landing normalizes policy" narrative that powered the 2024–25 expansion trade. 
Markets are pricing an 80% chance of a Fed rate hike by December
, which is deeply hostile to long-duration equities and speculative/unprofitable growth names. Consumer discretionary faces a squeeze from persistent energy/food price inflation eroding real spending power.

---

## ¶3 — WHAT TO WATCH

**1. July ISM Manufacturing & Services (July 1 & 3) + June Jobs Report (July 3):** A payroll print above +150K with another strong revision confirms the "no landing" scenario and makes an October Fed hike near-certainty. A miss (sub-100K) would crack the thesis and is the highest-probability near-term catalyst for a meaningful rates rally and equity rotation from defensives back to growth.

**2. Strait of Hormuz / Iran Deal Durability:** 
Geopolitical tensions resurfaced following an attack on a cargo ship near Oman in the Strait of Hormuz.
 Energy prices are the single most important variable governing headline PCE, the Fed's optionality, and consumer confidence. A re-escalation pushing oil back above $90–$95/bbl would materially accelerate the case for Fed hikes and compress multiples. Any durable ceasefire and tanker flow normalization is the most powerful disinflationary force available — it's the bull market's clearest green light.

**3. June CPI (July 11) & Core PCE (July 25):** 
The Fed raised short-term inflation forecasts significantly, indicating that the current pace of cooling prices is slower than optimistic projections.
 These two prints are the Fed's permission slip. A second consecutive month of 0.2% MoM core CPI — as seen in May — combined with lower energy prices would de-price the hike cycle, compress real yields, and re-open the equity multiple expansion trade. A hot print (0.3%+ MoM core) validates the hawkish dot plot and likely precipitates spread widening in HY (currently at historically narrow 275 bps with little buffer) and a re-test of equity lows.

---
*Data as of June 26, 2026. Sources: FOMC June 2026 SEP, US Treasury, BLS, BEA, ICE BofA / FRED, GuruFocus, TradingEconomics.*

 The brand damage is quantifiably severe:

**Verdict: Expensive Cloud Platform Facing "Good Enough" AI Competition**

Snowflake's operational metrics are actually improving —

 However, the risk here is **valuation vs. competitive displacement**. Microsoft Fabric, Google BigQuery, and embedded LLM capabilities within hyperscalers are eroding SNOW's differentiation as a standalone data cloud. The stock trades at ~14–16x forward sales — expensive for a company whose 
non-GAAP product gross margin guidance stands at 75.0% and operating margin at just 13.5%.
 Net revenue retention of 126% is healthy but has been drifting down from 127% two years ago. The critical risk: hyperscalers bundling AI data services "for free" within existing cloud contracts, making the standalone Snowflake value proposition increasingly difficult to defend at premium pricing. **Blow-up trigger: NRR drops below 120%, or a major hyperscaler announces a bundled data cloud product targeting SNOW's installed base.**

Novo now expects adjusted sales and operating profit to decline 4–12% at constant exchange rates in 2026, clouded primarily by intensifying competition from Eli Lilly, U.S. pricing pressure, weaker injectable GLP-1 prescription trends, and reduced Medicaid obesity coverage.

**Verdict: Royalty Model Priced for a Monopoly That Doesn't Exist**

Google, NVIDIA, and multiple Chinese chipmakers are quietly building RISC-V-based designs that bypass ARM licensing entirely. 
Key risks for AI chipmakers include potential share-of-wallet losses to competitors and macro or regulatory shocks that could slow data-center spending.
 ARM also has a specific geopolitical overhang: SoftBank remains the majority owner with ~90% of float constrained, and any trade restriction on chip IP flowing to China (which represents ~25% of ARM royalties) would be a direct hit to revenue. **Blow-up trigger: Major hyperscaler announces RISC-V transition for data center, Apple shifts custom silicon IP licensing, or China export control escalation.**
